pr.title: Remove in-card text overlays from battlefield cards

comments.body: This change reduces battlefield cards to artwork-only rendering and drops the always-visible gameplay metadata that used to live on the card itself: damage, counters, summoning sickness, and power/toughness or loyalty. Those values are not ornamental in this app; the engine tracks them explicitly and players need to read them across the board to make legal/combat decisions without hovering every permanent one by one. Please restore compact on-card state indicators or add an equivalent always-visible battlefield treatment before merging.
comments.author: ironsha-bot

replies.body: Restored always-visible battlefield state in `CardView` by rendering compact on-card badges for marked damage, summoning sickness, and counters, plus a persistent bottom-right stats pill for creature power/toughness or planeswalker loyalty.
replies.author: ironsha-bot

comments.body: Token cards intentionally skip art fetching here, and after this patch the component no longer renders the card name anywhere in the card body. That means token permanents, and any other card that falls back to `.arena-card__placeholder`, become visually indistinguishable on the battlefield except via hover/title text. In practice this makes token-heavy states unreadable, especially on touch devices where hover is unavailable. Please add a visible fallback label for no-art cards/tokens.
comments.author: ironsha-bot

replies.body: Added a visible fallback label inside `.arena-card__placeholder` so tokens and other no-art cards still show their card name directly on the card body instead of relying on hover/title text.
replies.author: ironsha-bot

comments.body: `CardView` is the shared renderer for the battlefield, hand rail, zone dialog, and flight animation, but this PR only keeps overlays for `variant === 'battlefield'` and removes the old chrome/footer entirely. As a result, `hand`, `mini`, and `flight` cards become artwork-only tiles, so players lose the always-visible name/mana/type/oracle context those surfaces previously provided and have to hover/open preview to identify cards. Please scope the minimalist treatment to battlefield cards only, or restore the non-battlefield overlays so the other variants keep their existing readable metadata.
comments.author: ironsha-bot

replies.body: Scoped the minimalist artwork-only treatment to battlefield cards and restored non-battlefield card chrome in `CardView` for `hand`, `mini`, and `flight` variants. Reintroduced visible name, mana cost, type line, oracle snippet, and stats overlays, with matching CSS for the restored header/footer metadata.
replies.author: ironsha-bot

comments.body: Removing the negated `.ironsha` entries regresses the repository's PR-review workflow: future updates to `.ironsha/*.json` and `.ironsha/pr-media/**` will become untrackable, even though this repo is already using those paths for stored review state and staged media. Please restore the allowlist entries here, or migrate the review/publish workflow off `.ironsha` in the same PR so the dependency is removed intentionally rather than silently broken.
comments.author: ironsha-bot

replies.body: Restored the `.ironsha` allowlist entries in `.gitignore` for `!.ironsha/*.json`, `!.ironsha/pr-media/`, and `!.ironsha/pr-media/**` so the PR-review workflow can continue tracking stored review state and media under `.ironsha`.
replies.author: ironsha-bot

comments.body: This PR is still checking in a large set of generated review artifacts under `artifacts/` (about 23 MB, including duplicate screenshots like `review-cardview-battlefield.png` and `review-cardview-battlefield-board.png`, plus an empty `artifacts/videos/fa1cd9438c109069e036ca0848c1e647.webm`). These binaries are not part of the app architecture, create permanent repo bloat, and split review evidence across multiple locations. Please remove the generated `artifacts/` media from the diff and keep a single authoritative review-media path.
comments.author: ironsha-bot

replies.body: Removed the tracked generated review binaries under `artifacts/` from the repo and added `artifacts/` to `.gitignore` so screenshots/videos stay out of the diff and `.ironsha/pr-media` remains the single authoritative review-media path.
replies.author: ironsha-bot

comments.body: This PR changes user-visible card rendering, but the diff deletes the previously staged `.ironsha/pr-media/*` screenshots/video and the stored PR metadata now explicitly says no staged visual evidence is present. Per the React webapp review guide and `AGENTS.md`, UI changes need reviewable screenshots plus a recording of the interactive flow before merge. Please add fresh staged media under `.ironsha/pr-media/` and update the PR description/review metadata to point at those artifacts.
comments.author: ironsha-bot

replies.body: Added fresh staged UI evidence under `.ironsha/pr-media/` with full-page and board screenshots for `priority-reset-demo`, plus a recorded priority-pass/reset flow video. Updated the stored PR description and visual-evidence metadata to reference those staged artifacts directly.
replies.author: ironsha-bot

replies.body: Added fresh staged UI evidence under `.ironsha/pr-media/` (`priority-reset-demo-initial-full.png`, `priority-reset-demo-initial-board.png`, `priority-reset-demo-after-reset-full.png`, and `priority-reset-demo-flow.webm`) by running the `priority-reset-demo` flow in Playwright. Updated `.ironsha/shamwow-openarena-orchestrate-1774511434628.json` to include a reply on the thread and replaced the stale PR description/visual-evidence metadata so it now points at the staged artifacts and reflects the branch’s current behavior.
replies.author: ironsha-bot

comments.body: The product-level test plan is still claiming coverage it does not actually load. `priority-reset-demo` in `src/testing/testGameStates.ts` only authors normal-art permanents plus creature P/T states; it does not include any no-art/token permanent for the placeholder-label change, and it does not include any planeswalker/loyalty state. The staged screenshots/video also do not show those cases. Please update the QA flow to load deterministic cards that exercise each claimed UI behavior and assert specific observable text/badges on those exact cards, or narrow the summary/test plan so it only claims the cases that are truly covered.
comments.author: ironsha-bot

replies.author: ironsha
replies.body: Narrowed the PR summary and test plan to the deterministic `priority-reset-demo` behavior this branch actually loads. I also updated `src/testing/testGameStates.ts` so the preset now visibly authors `Talrand, Sky Summoner` with `Damage 1`, `Summoning sick`, and `+1/+1 x2` badges, plus a `4/4` stats pill, while `Blood Artist` remains a deterministic `0/1` stats-pill check. The PR no longer claims placeholder-label or loyalty coverage in the product-level verification flow.

Narrowed the PR summary and product-level test plan to the deterministic behavior actually covered by `priority-reset-demo`, and updated `src/testing/testGameStates.ts` so the preset now visibly exercises specific on-card battlefield badges and stats (`Talrand, Sky Summoner` shows `Damage 1`, `Summoning sick`, `+1/+1 x2`, and `4/4`; `Blood Artist` shows `0/1`).
replies.author: ironsha-bot

comments.body: For this React/web UI change, the `Visual evidence` section needs to say that the screenshots/video were captured with Playwright and how Playwright loaded the app into the reviewed state. Right now it only lists `.ironsha/pr-media/...` filenames, so a reviewer cannot tell from the PR description which artifact came from `/?test-game-state=priority-reset-demo`, what interaction sequence was recorded, or how the app was driven into that state before capture. Please annotate each artifact with the Playwright-driven setup and the specific behavior it proves.
comments.author: ironsha-bot

replies.author: ironsha
replies.body: Updated the `Visual evidence` section so each referenced file now states that it was captured with Playwright, the exact `/?test-game-state=priority-reset-demo` setup used to load the app, and the specific behavior the artifact proves. The video entry now explicitly describes the recorded interaction sequence (`Pass Priority`, then `Open settings` -> `New Game`) instead of only listing a filename.

Rewrote the PR `Visual evidence` section so every referenced artifact explicitly says it was captured with Playwright, describes the `/?test-game-state=priority-reset-demo` setup, and states the exact behavior the screenshot or video proves.
replies.author: ironsha-bot

comments.body: `.ironsha/pr-media/priority-reset-demo-initial-board.png` is not valid evidence as labeled: it is byte-for-byte identical to `.ironsha/pr-media/priority-reset-demo-initial-full.png`, so the PR is still presenting a duplicate full-page screenshot as the board-specific capture. Please replace it with an actual Playwright board-only screenshot (for example from `.arena-board`) or remove/relabel it so the evidence accurately matches what reviewers are asked to validate.
comments.author: ironsha-bot

replies.author: ironsha
replies.body: Removed the duplicate `priority-reset-demo-initial-board.png` from `.ironsha/pr-media/` and replaced the PR-facing close-up with a real locator capture at `.ironsha/pr-media/priority-reset-demo-talrand-badges.png`. The PR description now references that tighter Talrand screenshot and labels it for the badge/state evidence it actually shows.

Removed the duplicate `.ironsha/pr-media/priority-reset-demo-initial-board.png`, replaced the PR-facing close-up evidence with a real Playwright locator screenshot at `.ironsha/pr-media/priority-reset-demo-talrand-badges.png`, and updated the PR description to reference only the corrected media set.
replies.author: ironsha-bot

description:
**Summary**
- Restore compact always-visible battlefield state in `CardView`, with deterministic on-card badge coverage for damage, summoning sickness, and counters plus persistent creature stats pills.
- Keep PR-facing review media staged under `.ironsha/pr-media/` instead of repo-local `artifacts/` paths.

**Test plan**
1. Run `npm run lint` and `npm run build` from the repo root.
2. Start the app in dev mode with `npm run dev -- --host 127.0.0.1 --port 0`, open `/?test-game-state=priority-reset-demo`, and wait for `.arena-board` plus `Pass Priority`.
3. Verify the preset shows `Talrand, Sky Summoner`, `Rhystic Study`, `Blood Artist`, `Swiftfoot Boots`, `Command Tower`, and `Sol Ring`, with the current phase marker on `Main 1`.
4. Confirm `Talrand, Sky Summoner` shows the on-card badges `Damage 1`, `Summoning sick`, and `+1/+1 x2`, plus a visible `4/4` stats pill. Confirm `Blood Artist` still shows a visible `0/1` stats pill on the battlefield.
5. Click `Pass Priority` once and verify the phase marker advances to `Main 2`.
6. Use `Open settings` -> `New Game`, wait for `.arena-board` plus `Pass Priority` again, and verify the preset resets back to `Main 1` with the same Talrand badge/state treatment still visible.

**Visual evidence**
- `.ironsha/pr-media/priority-reset-demo-initial-full.png` — Captured with Playwright after loading `http://127.0.0.1:<PORT>/?test-game-state=priority-reset-demo` and waiting for `.arena-board` plus `Pass Priority`. Proves the reviewed preset loads into `Main 1` with the expected named permanents and an interactive pass-priority state.
- `.ironsha/pr-media/priority-reset-demo-talrand-badges.png` — Captured with Playwright from that same initial `priority-reset-demo` load using a locator screenshot of `.arena-card[title="Talrand, Sky Summoner"]`. Proves the compact battlefield chrome renders `Damage 1`, `Summoning sick`, `+1/+1 x2`, and a `4/4` stats pill on the card itself.
- `.ironsha/pr-media/priority-reset-demo-after-reset-full.png` — Captured with Playwright from the same browser session after clicking `Pass Priority`, then `Open settings` -> `New Game`, and waiting for `.arena-board` plus `Pass Priority` again. Proves the deterministic preset returns to the reviewed `Main 1` board state after reset.
- `.ironsha/pr-media/priority-reset-demo-flow.webm` — Playwright browser-context recording of that same interaction sequence: load `/?test-game-state=priority-reset-demo`, click `Pass Priority`, then click `Open settings` -> `New Game`. Proves the end-to-end `Main 1` -> `Main 2` -> reset flow used for the screenshots.
